system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

        nuInf   10;
        m       0.4;
        n       3;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

viscosityModel  constant;

nu              1e-03;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    cylinder
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    cylinder
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //
'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    (-1.000000 -0.675000 -0.500000)   // 0
    (-1.000000  0.325000 -0.500000)   // 1
    ( 0.325000 -0.675000 -0.500000)   // 2
    ( 0.325000  0.325000 -0.500000)   // 3
    ( 1.000000 -0.675000 -0.500000)   // 4
    ( 1.000000  0.325000 -0.500000)   // 5

    (-1.000000 -0.675000  0.500000)   // 6
    (-1.000000  0.325000  0.500000)   // 7
    ( 0.325000 -0.675000  0.500000)   // 8
    ( 0.325000  0.325000  0.500000)   // 9
    ( 1.000000 -0.675000  0.500000)   // 10
    ( 1.000000  0.325000  0.500000)   // 11
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: lower-left
    hex (0 1 7 6 2 3 9 8) (18 18 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: upper-left
    hex (1 5 11 7 3 9 13 13) (18 18 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: lower-right
    hex (2 3 9 8 4 5 11 10) (36 18 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: upper-right
    hex (3 5 11 9 6 10 14 14) (36 18 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
    arc 2 3 (0.325 0 0)
    arc 8 9 (0.325 0 0)
    arc 6 7 (0.325 0 0)
    arc 10 11 (0.325 0 0)
);

sets
(
    cylinderFace
    {
        type vertexSet;
        points
        (
            (2 3 8 9)
        );
    }
);

patchInfo
(
    cylinder
    {
        type madeFrom;
        source  cylinderFace;
        operation makePatch;
    }
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 6 7 1)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (4 10 11 5)
            (5 11 13 7)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 11 7)
            (5 11 13 7)
            (3 9 13 13)
            (9 13 14 14)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 2 8 6)
            (2 4 10 8)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            // z = -0.5
            (0 1 3 2)
            (2 3 9 8)
            (4 5 11 10)
            (6 7 9 8)
            // z = 0.5
            (3 7 9 9)
            (9 13 14 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //
')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Point Label Out of Range

**Error Message:**
```
Point label 13 out of range 0..11 in block hex (1 5 11 7 3 9 13 13) (18 18 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1))
```

**Diagnosis:**

The block references vertex 13, but only 12 vertices (0–11) are defined. This has persisted across all 8 attempts. The fundamental problem is that the blockMeshDict defines 4 blocks that collectively reference more than 12 unique vertices, but only 12 vertices are declared.

Looking at the current blockMeshDict carefully, the blocks reference vertices 12, 13, 14, 15 which don't exist. Previous attempts added more vertices but the block topology kept changing. The current attempt tries to reuse the existing 12 vertices (0–11) but the block definitions map them incorrectly.

The block `hex (1 5 11 7 3 9 13 13)` references vertex 13 twice and misses vertices that should be there. The issue is that the block topology is fundamentally incompatible with the available vertices.

**Resolution — Radically simplify the blockMeshDict:**

Given that this is a simple channel with a single cylindrical obstacle, use a **single-block** approach with a hole. Declare only 12 vertices (0–11) as currently defined, and define just ONE hex block that covers the entire domain except the cylinder region. Use the `hole` mechanism to carve out the cylinder:

1. Define a single hex block covering the full domain bounding box using all 12 vertices.
2. Create a `vertexSet` called `cylinderHole` containing the 4 vertices that form the cylinder face (vertices 2, 3, 8, 9 — these are the square face of the cylinder).
3. Apply `makePatch` to convert `cylinderHole` into the `cylinder` boundary patch.
4. Define the remaining outer boundary patches: `inlet`, `outlet`, `top`, `bottom`, `frontAndBack`.
5. Set `nCells` to something reasonable like `(18 18 1)`.

The single hex block vertices should be ordered to cover the full domain: front face `(0 1 3 2)` and back face `(4 5 7 6)` — but this needs to be done carefully so the block isn't inside-out. Actually, with 12 vertices, the block would need 8 vertices. The cleanest approach is to define the block as covering the full domain with a hole punch:

- Single block: `hex (0 1 5 4 2 3 7 6)` — this covers the full L-shaped domain excluding the cylinder interior.
- Cylinder hole vertices: `{2, 3, 8, 9}` — but vertices 8,9 are at x=0.325 which is inside the domain. The hole face should be the square face of the cylinder at x=0.325, i.e., vertices 2,3,9,8.

Wait — vertices 2,3 are at x=0.325,y=-0.675/-0.325 and vertices 8,9 are at x=0.325,y=-0.675/+0.325. These form the left face of the cylinder hole. The block `hex (0 1 5 4 2 3 7 6)` with the hole at vertices 2,3,8,9 should work.

## Error 2: `log.pimpleFoam` — Cannot Find Mesh

**Diagnosis:**

This is a cascading error. Since `blockMesh` fails, no mesh exists for `pimpleFoam` to read.

**Resolution:**

Fix the `blockMeshDict` by implementing the single-block-with-hole approach described above. Use only the 12 existing vertices (0–11), define a single hex block with appropriate vertex ordering, and use `sets` + `makePatch` to create the cylinder hole. Remove all 4 block definitions and replace with one block. Update the `boundary` section accordingly.

## Summary

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Rewrite completely. Use only the 12 existing vertices (0–11). Define a single hex block (e.g., `hex (0 1 5 4 2 3 7 6)`) that spans the full domain. Create a `vertexSet` named `cylinderHole` containing `{2, 3, 8, 9}` and apply `makePatch` to turn it into the `cylinder` boundary patch. Define `inlet`, `outlet`, `top`, `bottom`, `cylinder`, and `frontAndBack` patches in the `boundary` section. Use `line` edges for all edges. Remove the `sets` block that lists `cylinderFace` — instead define the hole vertices directly inline or use a simpler set definition.
2. **`0/U` and `0/p`**: Ensure all patches from the new blockMeshDict are covered (`inlet`, `outlet`, `top`, `bottom`, `cylinder`, `frontAndBack`). Remove any `defaultFaces` entries.
3. Re-run `blockMesh` then `pimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace all 4 block definitions with a single hex block definition: hex (0 1 5 4 2 3 7 6) (18 18 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Replace the sets block entirely with a vertexSet named cylinderHole containing points (2 3 8 9); Add a patch entry in boundary for cylinder using makePatch from cylinderHole; Keep inlet, outlet, top, bottom, and frontAndBack patches unchanged; Remove the old cylinderFace set block entirely'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-03 in physical properties.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
